9KD5-7FS: Simon Hadley Sr. was born in County Westmeath, Ireland, in 1640, and lived his life in the Irish midlands, with residences at Ballynakill in County Offaly and at Moate. From about 1670 to 1700 he ran a fishery and iron foundry in County Dublin. A member of the Society of Friends, he worshipped as a Quaker at Moate in Kilcleagh, and he died in his native County Westmeath in 1711, his estate passing through probate that same year.
MJL5-ZCM: Jean-Baptiste Joffrion was born in 1671 at Chambly, near Montreal, in the colony of New France. He lived and died within that French Canadian community, passing away at Varennes in 1740.
L8MQ-54Z: Hannah Scott was born in England around 1635, though her exact birthplace remains uncertain because it is not clear when her father first came to America. She crossed the Atlantic and made her home in colonial Massachusetts, where she died at Braintree in 1718.
LCXD-7T9: Thomas Blunt was born in 1656 in Surry, Virginia, the only son of Richard Blunt. In March 1677, having come of age, he sold the property he had inherited from his late father. Over the years he accumulated land, and in 1714 he patented hundreds of acres of new land along the Nottoway River and Whitewood Swamp near the line of the Nottoway Indians. He drew up his will in 1708, naming his wife Priscilla executor, and after his death in 1709 the will was probated, leaving land to John and Mary Flood, Howell Edmunds, and Joel Barker; his son Thomas Blunt Jr. later served as executor of the estate.
KN7K-FMN: Johannes Georg Martin Anthis Bender was born in 1631 and baptized that year in the Baden region of Germany, in the parishes of Eschelbach near Sinsheim and Nußloch near Heidelberg. He lived a short life in Germany, dying in 1672.
KNN2-YW6: Barbara Jausslin was born in 1646 at Thürnen in the Swiss canton of Basel-Landschaft. She was baptized in 1667 and made her home at nearby Sissach.
LCF9-X6Q: Peter Webster was born in 1650 at Ingleton in Yorkshire, England, and later resided in Buckinghamshire. In November 1695 he immigrated to Middletown in Bucks County, Pennsylvania, and he died there in the colony, at Bristol, in 1724.
LCRW-4XM: Thomas Whicker Sr. was born in England in 1652. In 1685 he came to colonial Virginia under an indenture, agreeing to four years of service in exchange for his passage. In 1689 he went to court seeking release from that indenture, and in the years that followed he witnessed a will and saw to his own affairs. He wrote his will in April 1704 in Westmoreland County, Virginia, where it was probated after his death later that summer.
GGTD-GH3: Elizabeth Catlett was born in 1708 in Stafford, Virginia, in British Colonial America. She lived her life in colonial Virginia and died in Fairfax County in 1763.
M11B-CJ3: Julianne Henrietta Grimm was born in 1784 in the Palatinate region of Bavaria, Germany, and died in 1828.
LKPQ-5TG: Anna Barbara Finck was born in 1663 at Kusel in Bavaria, where she also made her home. She died in 1716 at Baumholder in the Rhineland.
LYX9-GRR: Margaret Mansdotter Lom was born in 1626 in Roslagen, in the Stockholm region of Sweden. She crossed the Atlantic to the Swedish and English settlements along the Delaware, and in August 1701 she made her will at Kingsessing in Pennsylvania. She died in Philadelphia in 1703.
G32F-NF4: Johann Windel Muller, also recorded as Wendel Müller and Johann Windle Miller, was born in 1721 at Niederlinxweiler in the Saarland region of Germany. He emigrated to colonial America and settled on the frontier at Patterson Creek in what is now Mineral County, West Virginia. There, in August 1761, he was killed, shot by Indians.
LZXM-X6Y: Mary Flood was born in 1630 in Surry, Virginia, in British Colonial America, and lived her life in the colony, dying there in 1681.
LRRK-T2K: Heinrich Gerster was born in 1688 in Basel, Switzerland, and resided at Sissach in Basel-Landschaft. In 1736 he emigrated from Switzerland to the Carolina colony, settling on the American frontier. He died in Rowan County, North Carolina, in 1758.
LZF3-GNN: James Nisbet was born in 1667 at Hamilton in Lanarkshire, Scotland, where he spent his early life. He died in 1728 in Edinburgh.
LRRV-Z94: Francoise Benard was born in 1628 at Vouvray-sur-Loir in the Sarthe region of France. A Roman Catholic, she crossed the Atlantic to the French colony at Montreal, where she appeared in the censuses of 1667 and 1681. She died in 1707 at the Hôtel-Dieu in Ville-Marie, Montreal.
L2SD-DX9: Alice Stackhouse was born in 1699 at Langhorne in Middletown Township, Bucks County, Pennsylvania. She married in January 1715 in the same township and died in Philadelphia in 1764.
97QV-JYW: Elizabeth Carr was born in 1682 in Newport, Rhode Island, in British Colonial America. She lived a short life there and died in Newport in 1709.
LT86-2SW: Elizabeth Travis was born in 1667 at Gloucester in Essex County, Massachusetts Bay Colony. She lived her life in colonial Massachusetts and died at Lynn in 1746.
L89J-BMQ: Hanss Jerg Voglemann was born in 1699 at Westheim in Württemberg, Germany. He died in 1758 at Öhringen in his native Württemberg.
GWXZ-T9J: Sir George Hog was born in 1598 in Midlothian, Scotland, and held the title of 7th Baron of Bemerside. He resided at Edinburgh and died in the Lothian region of Scotland in 1666.
LX9M-4NN: Robert Longshore was born in 1655 at Wigan in Lancashire, England, and worked as a surveyor. He immigrated to Pennsylvania before 1681, and as Deputy Surveyor he drew up the plans and survey of Philadelphia in 1682. He continued to live in the colony, presenting a petition in 1692, and died in Philadelphia in 1694.
G494-PDY: William Weeks was born in 1620 at Staines in Middlesex, England. He crossed the Atlantic to colonial Massachusetts, where he made his living as a ship owner, trader, and tavern keeper. He died at Edgartown on Martha's Vineyard in 1689.
LCV9-3C9: Wynand Pieterszn van Eck was born in 1640 at Eck en Wiel in Gelderland, in the Netherlands. He immigrated to the New World, reaching New York around 1661 and arriving again in 1664, and in 1679 he stood as a witness at the Dutch Church near Rensselaer. He made his home in Kings County and died at Brooklyn in 1690.
M3KW-4NF: Frances Buckner was born in 1675 in Old Rappahannock County, Virginia, in British Colonial America. She lived her life in colonial Virginia, drawing up her will in January 1743 in King George County, where it was proved in 1745 following her death.
LBD8-JHW: Thomas Perry was born in 1691 at Bilston in Staffordshire, England, and was baptized that May at Wolverhampton. He lived in England and died at Winterbourne in Gloucestershire in 1744.
LYWV-JH5: Thomas Hicks was born in 1640 in Rhode Island, in British Colonial America, and lived a long life that spanned nearly a century. He served in the militia in Queens around 1686 and made his home at Flushing in the New York colony, where he died in 1739.
